Title: Noncatalytic chalcone isomerase-fold proteins in Humulus lupulus are auxiliary components in prenylated flavonoid biosynthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Humulus lupulus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in Arabidopsis/protoplast assays; GAL promoters in yeast, inducible by galactose
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances DMX and flavonoid production by interacting with CHS_H1 and PT1L and increasing their catalytic efficiency
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop cones
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive prenylchalcone precursor to xanthohumol with pharmaceutical value
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction; Y2H; split-ubiquitin MbY2H; luciferase complementation; co-IP; in vitro enzyme assays; enzyme kinetics; Arabidopsis overexpression/metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis tt5-1 overexpression lines increased NC accumulation; in hop trichomes endogenous expression is trichome-specific
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with p-coumarate in yeast; galactose induction; Arabidopsis seedling growth on 1/2 MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS_H1; HlPT1L; CHS proteins from Arabidopsis, rice, Selaginella, and Physcomitrella
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No prenylated flavonoids detected in Arabidopsis expressing PT1L or CHIL2-PT1L despite PT1L mRNA; no major penalty reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Noncatalytic protein lacking canonical CHI activity; conserved CHS-enhancing function across land-plant type IV CHI-fold proteins; considered part of active DMX biosynthetic metabolon
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HlCHIL2 is a noncatalytic type IV CHI-fold protein specifically expressed in hop trichomes and localized in the cytoplasm. 2. Introducing HlCHIL2 into the reconstructed yeast pathway increased total DMX production about 2.3-fold and N/NC production about 1.3-fold. 3. HlCHIL2 physically interacted with CHS_H1 and HlPT1L and increased the catalytic efficiency of both enzymes. 4. HlCHIL2 overexpression increased NC accumulation in Arabidopsis tt5-1, supporting an in planta CHS-enhancing role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong auxiliary component for metabolic engineering of prenylchalcones/flavonoids, especially by coexpression with CHS and PT enzymes to build productive metabolons
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Hop trichome EST library; qRT-PCR dataset S3; public RNA-seq resources mentioned, accession NA

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in protoplast assays; GAL promoters in yeast, inducible by galactose
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III noncatalytic CHI-fold protein; FAP1-related polyketide-binding protein candidate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases DMX production in engineered yeast and binds DMX and naringenin chalcone to stabilize their ring-open structures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop cones
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive prenylchalcone precursor to xanthohumol with pharmaceutical value
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction; protein-metabolite binding assay; differential scanning fluorimetry; homology modeling and docking; mutagenesis of binding-pocket residues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with p-coumarate in yeast; 4°C incubation in binding assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced total DMX production by about 30% in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No CHI catalytic activity detected; no interaction with CHIL2, PT1L, CCL1, CHS_H1, or OMT1 was detected; authors propose HlCHIL1 homologs as polyketide-binding proteins (PBP)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HlCHIL1 is a noncatalytic type III CHI-fold protein specifically expressed in hop trichomes and localized in the cytoplasm. 2. Expression of HlCHIL1 in the reconstructed yeast pathway reduced total DMX production by about 30%. 3. HlCHIL1 binds DMX and naringenin chalcone, but not naringenin, 6PN, 8PN, or XN, and stabilizes the ring-open chalcone conformation. 4. Binding-pocket residues V139 and A143 jointly contribute to DMX/NC binding, supporting a specialized polyketide-binding function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be exploited for metabolite stabilization or sequestration strategies, but may reduce recoverable DMX in microbial production systems
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Hop trichome EST library; qRT-PCR dataset S3; public RNA-seq resources mentioned, accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters in pESC vectors, inducible by galactose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a prenyl group from DMAPP to naringenin chalcone to form demethylxanthohumol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop cones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical bioactivity-associated prenylchalcone precursor to xanthohumol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for DMX production in reconstructed yeast; PT1L alone was sufficient for the prenylation step in the DMX pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction; metabolite profiling by LC-MS; microsomal enzyme kinetics; split-ubiquitin interaction assay; luciferase complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Trichome-associated in hop glandular trichomes based on pathway context from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with p-coumarate; galactose induction in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; key catalytic prenylation step for DMX formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PT2 could not produce DMX in the reconstruction and adding PT2 did not improve DMX production; CHIL2 improved PT1L kinetic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HlPT1L alone catalyzed the prenylation step required for DMX formation in the reconstructed yeast pathway. 2. HlPT1L physically interacted with HlCHIL2. 3. The PT1L/CHIL2 complex showed improved kinetic performance relative to PT1L alone.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for microbial or plant engineering of prenylchalcone production, especially together with HlCHIL2 to improve flux
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hop trichome EST library; public RNA-seq resources mentioned, accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters in pESC vectors, inducible by galactose
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core chalcone-forming step feeding prenylchalcone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to produce naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop cones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces naringenin chalcone precursor for DMX; its catalytic efficiency is enhanced by HlCHIL2, increasing NC and DMX output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; Y2H; luciferase complementation; co-IP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Trichome-specific CHS involved in XN biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with p-coumarate; galactose induction in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by CHIL2-mediated activity enhancement, but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Type IV CHI-fold proteins from multiple land plants physically interacted with CHSs and enhanced activity 1.5- to 3-fold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS_H1 generated the naringenin chalcone precursor required for DMX biosynthesis. 2. HlCHIL2 physically interacted with CHS_H1. 3. CHIL2 increased CHS_H1 catalytic efficiency and elevated NC and DMX production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central enzyme to stack with HlCHIL2 and HlPT1L for improved prenylchalcone production in metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hop trichome EST library; public RNA-seq resources mentioned, accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters in pESC vectors, inducible by galactose
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenylpropanoid activation step feeding chalcone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop cones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as upstream precursor-supplying enzyme in reconstructed DMX pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with p-coumarate; galactose induction in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in pathway reconstruction but not a primary mechanistic focus of this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCL1 was part of the reconstructed DMX pathway in yeast. 2. It supplies p-coumaroyl-CoA needed by CHS_H1. 3. No specific new mechanistic role beyond pathway reconstitution was shown here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary upstream enzyme for pathway reconstruction and precursor supply in engineering systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Hop trichome EST library; public RNA-seq resources mentioned, accession NA
